title: Pinned Transform Rules (parse_rules.yaml)
description: Shape of ledger/parse_rules.yaml — the human-readable, diff-able SOURCE OF TRUTH for human-pinned transform-cache rules (the Tier-3 value cache). Each rule maps an exact input string for a given transform to an authoritative, VERSION-INDEPENDENT output that wins over any derived (deterministic/llm) entry and survives every transform-version bump. Seeded into ledger/transform_cache.sqlite via TransformCache.seed_from_rules() and exported back via export_pins(). See 01-operating-model.md §5.3 and scripts/transform_cache.py.

pinned_rules.description: One entry per pinned (transform, input [, context_signature]) → output.

transform.description: The cleansing primitive's name (e.g. "parse_name", "normalize_date"). Part of the cache key; pinned rules are matched ignoring transform_version.

input.description: The exact source string this rule resolves (NFC + whitespace-collapsed on lookup). e.g. "St. John-Smith, Rev. Dr. A.".

context_signature.description: Column-level decisions that change the output (e.g. "name_order=last_first", "date_format=MDY"). Empty/omitted for context-free transforms. Part of the key, so a rule only applies under the matching interpretation.

output.description: The authoritative parsed value — the structured object the transform produces (name parts, partial-date dict, etc.). Stored verbatim as the cache entry's value.

note.description: Why this rule was pinned (free-form audit note).

pinned_by.description: Operator email or identifier who pinned the rule.
